document_id: How to Review Submissions using the eNC3 Portal

findings.description: Top logo is smaller and shifted left in the HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF shows the NCDOR logo centered near the top, while the HTML places it smaller and left-aligned above the title.

findings.description: Overall spacing and section positioning differ noticeably from the PDF.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF has centered title treatment and more balanced top whitespace; the HTML uses a left-aligned title with a horizontal rule and tighter section spacing, altering the page structure but not the content.

findings.description: Heading alignment and visual emphasis are changed in the HTML.
findings.evidence: The page 1 title is centered in the PDF but left-aligned in the HTML, and the section heading styling differs, though headings remain distinct.

findings.description: Annotated instructional graphic is clipped in the HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 2, the PDF shows the full 'Uploaded Files' callout image with red labels and arrows; in the HTML, the left side and lower labels are cut off, making parts of the graphic illegible.

findings.description: Graphic callout labels were partially converted into separate body text lines.
findings.evidence: On page 2, labels such as 'Total number of files uploaded', 'File size and name', 'Form name', and 'Number of files uploaded for the form type' appear embedded around the annotated image in the PDF, but in the HTML they also appear as detached plain text lines below the clipped image.

findings.description: Lower package example image is partially cropped in the HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 2, the package example graphic near the bottom is fully visible in the PDF, while the HTML shows only the upper-left portion of that image.
